channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Graduate Student Lunch and Learn - Preparing for the academic/industry job market. Registration Required.
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 10:30 AM - 12:30 PM - Join us for faculty and staff to help you prepare for the academic/industry job market with their firsthand experience. Registration Required. RSVP to sanchn2@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: Dining and Flex Dollars Reminder
item.description: Friendly reminder to all students with active residential meal plans that the last day to purchase additional flex dollars for the Spring 2026 semester will be April 27th. The last day to utilize Flex Dollars for Spring 2026 meal plans is May 16th — please keep in mind that flex dollar balances do not carry over at the end of the academic year. Dining Halls close for the Spring 2026 semester on May 8th after dinner. For more information click the link above.
item.author: Students

item.title: We’re Hiring Our 2026-2027 Student Success Center Tutors
item.description: The Student Success Center is currently recruiting 2026-27 peer tutors across a variety of majors. This flexible, paid, on-campus role offers valuable experience in communication and leadership while supporting fellow students. Fall 2026 Success Center Applications are due May 15th: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_56fbplo7sUaJRgG
item.author: Students

item.title: Tutoring in Nason Classroom
item.description: Did you know we offer tutoring from our Success Center Tutors in Nason Hall? We offer Tutoring in the following courses in Nason: Physics I & II- Mondays, 7-9pm CAD - Mondays, 7-9pm CAL I & II -Tuesdays, 7-9pm CS I and Data Structures - Wednesdays, 7-9pm
item.author: Students

item.title: Poster Review with the Success Center’s Writing Lab
item.description: If you’re preparing for the Undergraduate Research Symposium or an upcoming conference, join us for a Poster Review Pizza Night hosted by the Writing Lab! Bring your poster draft and enjoy pizza while receiving personalized feedback on your visual design, content clarity, and overall presentation. Tuesday, April 14th 5-6pm Success Center: Classroom 107, Folsom Library
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Review Sessions: Week of 4/12-4/18
item.description: Week of 4/12- 4/18: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Thermodynamics: Sunday, 4/12 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Essence of Eng. Mechanics: Monday, 4/13 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Computer Science: Tuesday, 4/14 (7-9pm) with SASE in the Nason Classroom Diff Eq.: Wednesday, 4/15 (7-9pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Attention: First-Year Students & Graduating Seniors
item.description: Help Us Improve Your RPI Experience. Share Your Voice! Take the National Survey of Student Engagement (NSSE) survey to help RPI improve your educational experience. Check your email for your personal invitation to participate!
item.author: Students

item.title: Intellectual Property & Technology Licensing Seminar
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM - Are you working on groundbreaking research? Curious about how to protect, commercialize, or launch a start-up based on your innovations through IP? Join us for an exciting campuswide seminar hosted by the Office of Intellectual Property and Technology Licensing (IPTL) at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI)! This seminar is your chance to learn how IPTL supports faculty and students in translating research into real-world impact through…
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Colloquium: Dr. Malik Magdon-Ismail from Computer Science-RPI
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “Label Complexity in Machine Learning” by Dr. Malik Magdon-Ismail. He is a Professor of Computer Science at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, where he has been a faculty member since 2000. His research is in machine learning, complex systems, and decision-making from data.
item.author: Community

item.title: Summer Internship Opportunity in the Design Lab!
item.description: Project: Capstone Team Formation and Project Assignment Tool Work description: This project will develop an AI-powered software tool to automate and optimize the student-to-project assignment process. The intern will build ML/NLP-powered tools to extract student competencies from unstructured data, map them to project requirements, and automatically generate optimized student-project matches based on interests and experience — all supported by the Design Lab team. For more information: https:/…
item.author: Community

item.title: MANE Spring Seminar Series Continues with Bicentennial Leaders Advancing Science and Technology given by Roderic Pettigrew
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 10:30 AM - 11:30 AM - Bicentennial Leaders Advancing Science and Technology - “Bioengineering and the Human Healthspan”, Dr. Roderic Pettigrew, Robert A Welch Professor, Former System Vice Chancellor for Health and Inaugural Dean School of Medicine, Texas A&M University
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Classification Best Practices
item.description: Information Classification is the process of categorizing data based on it’s sensitivity, value, and criticality to organizations, ensuring appropriate security controls are applied. Please click above link to review our Information Classification Best Practices and Policy.
item.author: Community

item.title: CEE Seminar Series - Dr. Mohammed Abdellatef Assistant Professor Florida Atlantic University
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - “Rheological Characterization of 3D-Printable Concrete Materials” The modern state of construction has been exploring the use of additive manufacturing technologies, commonly referred to as concrete 3D-Printing, in order to optimize several factors impacting the time and cost aspects related to the construction process. Unlike conventional concrete materials, 3D-printed concrete is a pseudo-solid material that allows for layer extrusion…
item.author: Community

item.title: Ribbon-Cutting for the Gene Haas Manufacturing Teaching & Learning Foundry
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM - Ribbon-Cutting Ceremony for the Gene Haas Manufacturing Teaching & Learning Foundry, a newly renovated facility dedicated to educating the next generation of leaders and innovators in manufacturing through advanced, hands-on learning. This event will bring together representatives from the Gene Haas Foundation, RPI community, RPI alumni, industry partners, local educators, and community members to celebrate the opening of this innovative…
item.author: Community

item.title: ECSE Seminar: “When GenAI Meets Extended Reality: Enabling Scalable and Natural Interactions”
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Seminar: “When GenAI Meets Extended Reality: Enabling Scalable and Natural Interactions” with Prof. Bin Li of Pennsylvania State University. Abstract and bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: After Sustainability: Climate Change & Authoritarianism - Talk scheduled for April 23
item.description: How is the climate crisis reshaping politics? April Anson (University of Connecticut) and William Callison (Hunter College) join RPI for a public conversation on climate politics and the global rise of authoritarianism. Bringing together political theory and environmental humanities, this event explores the growing connections between climate crisis and far-right politics—and what that may mean for the future. Event starts at 5pm on April 23 in CBIS Auditorium.
item.author: Community

item.title: CAS SciFinder Featured Webinar: Agentic AI in CAS SciFinder
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 2:00 PM - 2:45 PM - Vendor-hosted webinar: Join us as we introduce CAS Newton℠, an agentic AI companion created to support the complex needs of scientific researchers. See how Science-Smart AI trained on the world’s most comprehensive database of curated scientific information delivers deep insights that general AI cannot produce reliably.
item.author: Community

item.title: Parking Lot Closure – Crane Staging April 16–17
item.description: Please be advised that, due to an HVAC project, a crane will be staged and in operation in the G-Lot from April 16 through 17. The G-Lot encompasses the front of Commons Dining Hall, the softball field area, the side of the Mueller Center, and the Department of Public Safety area. The affected parking areas will be closed and barricaded beginning the evening of April 15 and will remain unavailable for approximately 48 hours. Pedestrians are advised to use caution when traveling in the area due…
item.author: Community

item.title: MSE Seminar: Dr. Doug Chrisey
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - Photonic Processing of Defects in SiC for Quantum Applications Doug Chrisey Department of Physics Tulane University
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday April 17: Webex Event Training
item.description: Hosting a campus event or supporting a student webinar? Join a hands-on Webex Event Training Webinar, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. Register now for this webinar presented via Webex on Friday April 17 from 11am-12pm: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700 Learn the basics of Webex Event hosting, with demonstration and Q&A, along with new features recently added to the hosting platform.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Chemistry Seminar Series
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 12:00 AM - 1:00 AM - The Harteck Lecture Series in Physical Chemistry will feature guest speaker Prof. Dimitrios Pantazis, Max Planck Institute for Coal Research, who will speak on the topic Molecular Principles of Solar Energy Conversion in Photosynthesis.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop: “Getting Started with Percipio: Navigation & Q&A”
item.description:
This brief course is designed to help you get comfortable using Percipio, RPI’s digital learning platform. You’ll learn how to navigate the main dashboard, search for content, access assigned learning, and track your progress. We’ll also walk through common questions users have and show you where to find help when needed. Whether you’re brand new or just need a refresher, this session will ensure you’re ready to make the most of Percipio.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
